criteria_results.text: Press Space while playing -> playback stops, playhead stays at current position (does not jump to beat 1)
criteria_results.notes: Confirmed live: playhead stayed at 3.2.3 after Space. No divergence.

criteria_results.text: Press Space again -> playback resumes from the paused position
criteria_results.notes: Confirmed live: resumed from paused position, not from beat 1.

criteria_results.text: Stamping mid-song, pausing, and resuming preserves timeline position throughout
criteria_results.notes: Confirmed live: stamp + pause + resume cycle preserved position.

criteria_results.text: Existing play-from-beginning behavior (if needed) is accessible via a separate control or is intentionally removed
criteria_results.notes: Contract test passed (both stop_playing and set/current_song_time 0 were sent). Smoke found single 'Stop (to start)' reset the visual display to 1.1.1 but continue_playing resumed from the pre-stop position (~3.4). Root cause: stop_playing snapshots the current playhead as the internal resume point for continue_playing; setting current_song_time AFTER stop meant Ableton saved the old position, then the seek arrived too late. Double-clicking Stop worked (second stop found position already at 0 and saved 0 as resume point). Fix: reversed order — set/current_song_time 0 first, then stop_playing. Contract test did not enforce send order (used .toContain not positional assertion), so the wrong ordering was invisible to CI.

criteria_results.text: OSC command mapping implements pause-in-place: pause sends stop_playing (no rewind) and play sends continue_playing (resume from position), not start_playing
criteria_results.notes: Indirectly confirmed by c1/c2 smoke passing.

criteria_results.text: Preview tab renders chords above lyrics in the correct ChordPro style
criteria_results.notes: Smoke 7 scope was #30 playhead behavior only. #27 was verified in the prior verification-gate run (visual screenshot + Playwright e2e).

criteria_results.text: Directives ({title}, {comment}, {start_of_chorus}, etc.) render as section headers / labels
Stamping still uses lyric-only text (stamp log and .als clip names unchanged)
ChordPro text with no chords still renders correctly (pure lyric sheet)
Preview tab visibly shows chords above lyrics in the live app (rendered DOM)
criteria_results.notes: Playwright e2e covers this. Not re-run during smoke session.

criteria_results.text: The UI clearly communicates which line will be timestamped when the stamp button is pressed
After stamping, the newly-promoted line is clearly the 'next to stamp'
The distinction between 'just stamped' and 'about to stamp' is visually unambiguous
No actual stamp logic changes (only display/labelling)

observed_chain: smoke-test (Skill; walked Smoke 7 step-by-step with Ableton live)
inline fix (no failure-triage invoked; user approved fix directly in conversation)
contract tests only (npx vitest run --config vitest.contract.config.ts; 10/10 pass)

issues.description: When smoke found the stop-to-start bug, the fix was applied inline without routing through failure-triage. The user approved the fix direction conversationally, which is reasonable for a 2-line change, but the triage step (which would have produced a root-cause write-up and formal issue routing) was bypassed.
issues.detected_by: transcript inspection — no failure-triage Skill invocation before Edit call on osc-client.ts

issues.description: After applying the returnToStart() fix, only the 10 contract tests were run (not the full 145 unit + 52 e2e suite). The fix was confirmed working via re-smoke but without the full verification-gate evidence block. A one-line ordering change with direct contract test coverage is low risk, but the gate was not formally run.
issues.detected_by: transcript inspection — no full npm test or test:e2e run after the osc-client.ts edit

issues.description: project-state-updater was not invoked after the returnToStart fix. The fix should be reflected in the project-state test-status and implementation notes.
issues.detected_by: transcript inspection — no project-state-updater Skill invocation after fix

failure_output: Stop (to start) single press: app + Ableton display showed 1.1.1 but continue_playing resumed from ~3.4. Double-click workaround worked. Root: stop_playing saves current position as continue_playing resume point before set/current_song_time 0 could arrive. Fix: reversed order (set 0 then stop).
suggested_follow_up: The c4 contract test should assert send order (positional index check: setTime index < stopPlaying index) so future ordering regressions are caught headlessly rather than requiring a live Ableton smoke.
